title: epipactis helleborine subsp. leutei
intro: Epipactis helleborine subsp. leutei, also known as Leutel's helleborine orchid, is a subspecies of orchid native to Europe and Asia. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral activities, making it useful in treating various health issues. The plant is believed to have health benefits such as reducing fever, alleviating respiratory problems, and supporting immune function. The herbal preparations of Epipactis helleborine subsp. leutei include infusions, tinctures, and decoctions, which are often used to treat colds, coughs, and other minor health concerns.
